headline: Semiconductor Industry Faces 12,000 Engineer Shortage
context: Malaysia semiconductor industry reported 12,000 engineer shortage in 2025 threatening position as 6th largest chip packaging hub.

cards.big: 12,000 engineer shortage threatens Malaysia 6th-ranked semiconductor sector
cards.sub: Chip packaging hub status at risk as companies struggle to fill positions

cards.big: Universities produce 8,500 engineering grads but industry needs 15,000
cards.sub: Only 45% of grads enter semiconductor with rest choosing other fields

cards.big: Intel Infineon and 6 others offered 25% salary premiums to attract talent
cards.sub: Average semiconductor salary rose to RM8,500 up 35% from 2022

cards.big: Government launched RM500 million semiconductor talent programme in 2025
cards.sub: Programme covers 5,000 engineers but industry needs double annually

cards.big: Talent shortage tests whether Malaysia can retain its semiconductor industry position
12,000 engineer shortage in semiconductors. Talent pipelines take 5 years — investment must start now.
